frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: trial-by-trial value updating from reward feedback indexed in EEG

frameworks.name: working memory
frameworks.one_line: short-term storage of recent stimulus-action-outcome contingencies guiding choice

frameworks.name: dual-system decision-making
frameworks.one_line: RL and WM jointly contribute to learning with cooperative and competitive interactions

core_question: How do reinforcement learning and working memory systems interact within and across trials during human reward-based learning?
key_assumptions: trial-by-trial EEG signals can index latent RL and WM computations
manipulations: reward versus punishment feedback across trials
task structure permitting separable RL and WM contributions
key_findings: increases in neural expectation predicted reduced neural surprise at subsequent feedback
within- and cross-trial EEG dynamics show WM contributing expectations that guide RL
RL and WM compete during choice but cooperate during learning
interpretations: RL and WM cooperatively interplay for learning while competing at choice
single-trial neural indices reveal computations beyond what behavior alone can show
